goal: `yaptap` with no flags launches as a persistent macOS menu bar app. A global hotkey (⌥Space by default) starts and stops recording; the result is placed on the clipboard. Prompt selection is sticky via a menu dropdown.

in_scope: Mode detection: no-flags → app mode; any flag → existing CLI pipeline
Refactor shared audio capture, transcription, and LLM functions callable from both CLI and app mode
Config file (~/.config/yaptap/config.toml): read/write, hotkey, selected_prompt, whisper_model, llm_model
Single-instance guard via PID lock file
NSApplication in LSUIElement mode (no Dock icon)
Menu bar icon: two-state PNG template images (idle/active), three-bar equalizer design
Tray-icon dropdown menu: prompt list, No Prompt, hotkey display, Open Config, Quit
Sticky prompt selection: persisted to config file on each change
Global hotkey via rdev: configurable key+modifier, Accessibility permission handling
AppState machine: Idle → Recording → Processing → Idle
App-mode recording pipeline: audio → transcribe.py → llm.py (buffered) → clipboard
App-mode error handling: alert dialogs on audio/transcription/LLM failures
--device <index> CLI flag for explicit audio input device selection
Phase 3 error handling and exit codes

tasks.title: Add tray-icon, rdev, arboard to [dependencies] in Cargo.toml
At the top of main(): if no CLI flags are present (no prompt, prompt-file, list-prompts, model, llm-model, device), call run_app() and return; otherwise fall through to the existing CLI pipeline

Create src/audio.rs: extract cpal audio capture from main() into `fn capture_audio(device_index: Option<usize>) -> anyhow::Result<PathBuf>` returning the temp WAV path; update the CLI path in main() to call this function

Create src/transcription.rs: extract transcription subprocess spawn from main() into `fn run_transcription(wav_path: &Path, model: &str) -> anyhow::Result<String>` returning the transcript; update the CLI path in main() to call this function

Create src/llm.rs: add `fn run_llm_collect(transcript: &str, prompt_path: &Path, model: &str) -> anyhow::Result<String>` that reads all llm.py stdout into a String buffer and returns it (for app-mode clipboard output); keep the existing byte-streaming logic in main() for CLI mode
Add `--device <index: usize>` optional CLI flag to the existing Args struct; pass the value as Some(index) to capture_audio() in CLI mode

Create src/config.rs with `AppConfig` struct: fields hotkey (String), selected_prompt (String), whisper_model (String), llm_model (String); implement Default returning ("option+space", "", "base", "llama3")
Implement config file path helper in src/config.rs returning `~/.config/yaptap/config.toml` via the dirs crate (dirs::home_dir())
Implement AppConfig::load(): create ~/.config/yaptap/ directory if absent, write a default config.toml if absent, then parse the TOML file into AppConfig
In AppConfig::load(): on TOML parse error, show a macOS alert "Config file is not valid TOML: ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml — using defaults." and return AppConfig::default()

In AppConfig::load(): validate the hotkey field by attempting to parse it; on invalid value, show a macOS alert "Unknown hotkey '<value>' — using default: option+space." and reset the field to "option+space" in the returned struct
In AppConfig::load(): if selected_prompt is non-empty and no matching <stem>.toml exists in config/prompts/, silently reset it to "" in the in-memory struct (do not rewrite the file)

Implement AppConfig::save_prompt(stem: &str): write config.toml atomically (write full config to ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml.tmp then rename to config.toml), updating only selected_prompt; on write failure, log a warning to stderr and continue

On app launch (before NSRunLoop): if ~/.config/yaptap/yaptap.lock exists, read the stored PID and send signal 0 (kill -0); if the process is alive, exit 0 immediately; if dead (ESRCH), continue and overwrite the lock file
Write own PID as a decimal string to ~/.config/yaptap/yaptap.lock after the stale-lock check passes
Delete ~/.config/yaptap/yaptap.lock on clean exit: in the Quit YapTap handler and in a SIGTERM handler registered via ctrlc
Create src/app.rs with `pub fn run_app()` entry point; declare it in main.rs as `mod app`
In run_app(): call AppConfig::load() and store the result; perform single-instance lock file check and write
In run_app(): initialise NSApplication with activation policy Prohibited (LSUIElement) so no Dock icon or app menu bar appears
Create assets/icons/ directory

Create assets/icons/yaptap-idle.png (18×18px @1×): three filled black rectangles 3px wide with 2px gaps between them, heights left=6px centre=10px right=6px, centred in the 18×18 bounding box, on a transparent background (macOS template image format)
Create assets/icons/yaptap-idle@2x.png (36×36px @2×): same idle design at double resolution for Retina displays
Create assets/icons/yaptap-active.png (18×18px @1×): three filled black rectangles 3px wide with 2px gaps, all three bars 10px tall, centred in the 18×18 bounding box, transparent background
Create assets/icons/yaptap-active@2x.png (36×36px @2×): same active design at double resolution for Retina displays
In run_app(): create a tray-icon TrayIcon with the idle PNG loaded from assets/icons/yaptap-idle.png; set it as a template image so macOS adapts it to light and dark menu bar themes
Implement switch_icon(state: &AppState) helper: swap the TrayIcon image between idle and active PNG variants based on the current AppState

Implement build_menu(config: &AppConfig, prompts_dir: &Path) -> Menu: load all .toml files from config/prompts/ (binary-relative), sorted alphabetically by stem, and add one menu item per file showing the prompt's name field
In build_menu(): if config/prompts/ is absent or contains no .toml files, add a single disabled "No prompts found" item in place of the prompt list
In build_menu(): add a checkmark to the menu item whose stem matches config.selected_prompt; when selected_prompt is empty, add the checkmark to the "No Prompt" item instead
In build_menu(): add a separator after the prompt list, then a "No Prompt" item, then a second separator
In build_menu(): add a disabled hotkey display item showing the configured hotkey with Unicode modifier symbols (option→⌥, cmd→⌘, ctrl→⌃, shift→⇧, space→Space), e.g. "Hotkey: ⌥Space"
In build_menu(): add an "Open Config…" menu item followed by a separator and a "Quit YapTap" item
On "Open Config…" click: spawn `open ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml` as a subprocess
On "Quit YapTap" click: kill any in-progress transcription or LLM subprocess, delete temp WAV file if present, delete lock file, then call std::process::exit(0)
On prompt item click: update the in-memory selected_prompt to the clicked stem, call AppConfig::save_prompt(), then rebuild and set the updated menu
On "No Prompt" click: set selected_prompt to "", call AppConfig::save_prompt(""), then rebuild and set the updated menu
Rebuild the menu from disk (call build_menu()) each time the tray icon is clicked, so newly added or removed .toml files are reflected without restarting the app

Create src/hotkey.rs: implement `fn parse_hotkey(s: &str) -> anyhow::Result<(rdev::Key, Vec<rdev::Key>)>` that splits a "modifier+...+key" config string into a key and a list of required modifier keys
Check AXIsProcessTrusted() (via objc/core-foundation bindings) on app launch to determine whether Accessibility permission has been granted

If Accessibility permission is absent: show a macOS alert "YapTap needs Accessibility access to capture the global hotkey. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility?" with "Open Settings" and "Later" buttons; on "Open Settings" spawn `open "x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_Accessibility"`; skip hotkey registration

If Accessibility permission is granted: spawn a background thread calling rdev::listen() that filters events for the parsed hotkey key + modifiers and sends a HotkeyPressed message over an mpsc channel to the main thread

If rdev::listen() returns an error: show a one-time alert "The hotkey '<configured-hotkey>' could not be registered. Edit ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml to choose a different hotkey, then restart YapTap." (substitute the actual configured value, e.g. 'option+space')
Define `enum AppState { Idle, Recording, Processing }` in src/app.rs; hold the current state in an `Arc<Mutex<AppState>>` shared between the main thread and the hotkey listener thread
In the hotkey event handler: when state is Idle, send StartRecording over the mpsc channel; when state is Recording, send StopRecording; when state is Processing, do nothing
On StartRecording message: transition state to Recording and call switch_icon(Recording)
On StopRecording message: transition state to Processing (icon stays Active) and spawn the recording pipeline on a background thread
When the pipeline thread completes (success or error): send a PipelineDone message to the main thread; on receipt, transition state to Idle and call switch_icon(Idle)
In the pipeline thread: stop the in-progress cpal stream, flush the PCM buffer to a temp WAV file using the hound writer from src/audio.rs
Call run_transcription(wav_path, &config.whisper_model) from src/transcription.rs to obtain the transcript string; propagate errors to the error-handling path

If config.selected_prompt is non-empty: resolve the prompt TOML path as <binary_dir>/config/prompts/<selected_prompt>.toml and call run_llm_collect(transcript, prompt_path, &config.llm_model) from src/llm.rs to obtain the full LLM output string
If config.selected_prompt is empty ("No Prompt"): use the raw transcript string as the output text, skipping the LLM step
Write the output text to the system clipboard via arboard::Clipboard::new()?.set_text(output)
Delete the temp WAV file after the pipeline completes, whether the pipeline succeeded or failed
On audio capture error during recording (cpal stream error or device loss): transition to Idle, delete temp WAV if present, show a macOS alert dialog with the error message
On transcription failure in app mode (run_transcription returns Err): transition to Idle, delete temp WAV, show alert "Transcription failed — <error>"
On LLM failure in app mode (run_llm_collect returns Err): transition to Idle, delete temp WAV if present, show alert "LLM generation failed — <error>"
On "Quit YapTap" or SIGTERM while in Recording or Processing state: kill the in-progress transcription or LLM subprocess handle, delete temp WAV file, remove lock file, then exit 0
Verify cargo build succeeds with Phase 3 dependencies and passes cargo clippy -- -D warnings
Verify `yaptap` with no args shows the menu bar icon in idle state with the correct three-bar design
Verify the menu prompt list matches config/prompts/ sorted alphabetically and the checkmark reflects the persisted selected_prompt
Verify pressing the hotkey once switches the icon to active, pressing again runs the full pipeline, and the clipboard contains the LLM output (or raw transcript if No Prompt)
Verify `yaptap --prompt email-reply` still records, transcribes, and streams LLM output to stdout (Phase 2 CLI regression check)
